title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · Linux Operations Board
summary: Operate an unfamiliar simulated Linux host across filesystem, streams, identity, processes, packages, storage, services, networking, automation, security, and recovery without workshop prompts. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
